################################################################################
# batch_al.txt — gameplay C1->C2 drain (campaign batch 1 of ~5)
#
# Generated by the discover-c1-batch skill, 2026-06-02.
# Work type:    promote-c2 (C1 -> C2 mechanical promotion). AUTHOR-ONLY fanout.
# Sessions:     6  (Mashed_pool0,1,2,3,5,6 pre-assigned — pool4 SKIPPED, leak-locked)
# Total RVAs:   156  (gameplay, low-address slice 0x00405400..0x0041d910)
# Model:        Opus 4.8 (1M context)  (claude-opus-4-8[1m])
#               -> promote-c2 bulk-drain cap 60 RVAs/session (this batch: 26 each).
#               Same author-only cadence as batch_aj/ak (Opus author-only fanout
#               + scripted ghidra-sweep + central re-classify).
#
# CAMPAIGN CONTEXT: gameplay is a 792-RVA C1 pool — too big for one batch. This is
# batch 1 of ~5. It takes the LOWEST 156 by address (0x0040-0x0041), which is the
# core early game-logic region (boot/setup/race-init/menu glue — genuine game code,
# lowest library contamination). Later al-campaign batches walk up through the
# 0x0042/0x0044/0x0045/0x0055/0x0056/0x0057 gameplay clusters. render (458) is the
# OTHER remaining campaign, deferred (its 0x004e/0x0053/0x0054/0x0055 pages are
# RenderWare-core-heavy and need their own library-aware batches).
#
# Pre-flight (verified at generation time, 2026-06-02):
#   * re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Queued = EMPTY. batch_ak fully swept + re-classified
#     (sweep-20260603-0116; commits 221336aa + 39b5a2e1). vehicle + ai C1 = 0.
#     SAFE to fan out. (If a sweep is running when you start, STOP. Re-confirm.)
#   * Library-band screen over all 156 candidates: ZERO hits (CRT 0x004a0000-
#     0x004b3fff | D3DX9-PSGP 0x004ec000-0x004fc9e0 | qhull 0x0057c5b0-0x005a5820).
#     The slice is below all three bands. Still apply the library-confirm rule if
#     any RVA decodes as a named RenderWare/CRT primitive.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# AUTHOR-ONLY PROTOCOL (read before any session runs) — identical to batch_aj/ak
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   * DO write per-RVA .md plates into the session's bucket dir, using the
#     template heading **`## Mechanical description`** (NOT `## Purpose` — batch_aj
#     s3 used `## Purpose` and the sweep manifest builder returned 0 plated for
#     that bucket until renamed. This heading is a HARD requirement.)
#   * DO append ONE row to a PER-SESSION queue fragment:
#         re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_al_s<N>.md   (NOT the shared re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md)
#     The queued row MUST use the field name **`rvas=`** for everything the sweep
#     should plate (NOT `rvas_c2=` — batch_ak s3 used `rvas_c2=` and the builder
#     could not see it). If you have library-skips, put genuine-game RVAs in
#     `rvas=` and list vendored ones in a SEPARATE `library_skip=<rva>:<tag>,...`
#     field for the central re-classify (they are NOT plated by the sweep).
#   * DO NOT write hooks.csv. DO NOT run re-classify. DO NOT touch STUBS.md /
#     UNCERTAINTIES.md / DEFERRED.md / re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md directly.
#   * DO NOT mutate the master Ghidra project. Read-only MCP only (function_at,
#     decomp_function, listing_*). function_create / renames are the SWEEP's job —
#     if an RVA has no function object, write the plate from the raw listing and
#     set needs_function_create=<rva> in the queue row.
#   * DO NOT mint U-IDs or S-IDs. Mark new holes inline as bare `[UNCERTAIN]` in
#     the plate's `## Uncertainties` section with the gap described; central
#     re-classify mints from the reserved range (this batch: U-7700..U-7999,
#     S-6200..S-6399).
#   * Each session reads ONLY its pre-assigned pool clone (read-only). One commit
#     at end of session (plates + queue fragment), message:
#         "scribe(batch_al s<N>): author <K> C1->C2 plates for gameplay <range>"
#     Commit ATOMICALLY and path-scoped: `git commit -- <your bucket dir> <your
#     fragment>` — do NOT `git add -A`/`git add .` (a sibling parallel session may
#     have staged unrelated files; see memory feedback_parallel_fanout_gotchas #5,
#     the batch_aj-s5 shared-working-copy index race).
#
# DUPLICATE-INSTANCE GUARD (memory feedback_parallel_fanout_gotchas #7):
#   Before doing ANY work, check whether your bucket dir / fragment / pool marker
#   already exists (another terminal launched the same session). If so, STOP and
#   yield — do NOT overwrite. If you already wrote an early file that the owner
#   keeps, yield by `git restore`, NEVER blind-`rm`.
#
# FOLLOW-UP (single Opus session on main, AFTER all 6 commit — do NOT run now):
#   Scripted ghidra-sweep:
#     1. py -3.12 scripts/sweep_build_manifest.py --rows re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_al_s1.md
#        ... re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_al_s6.md --out sweep_manifest.json --date <D> --level C2
#        (confirm per-bucket plated == queued rva counts AND missing_md==0).
#     2. ghidra_eval apply via scripts/ghidra/sweep_apply_eval.py (one txn).
#     3. Central re-classify on main: C1->C2 for plated RVAs; reclass-OUT any
#        library_skip RVAs to third-party-library[...] (kept-C1); mint U-/S- IDs
#        from the reserved ranges. Merge fragments into SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Drained.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POOL-SLOT HYGIENE (every session)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_al_s<N>"      # per-session marker, no race
#   Use the PRE-ASSIGNED Mashed_pool<S> clone directly (do NOT call
#   ghidra_pool.ps1 acquire). Mashed_pool4 is SKIPPED (leaked in-JVM lock; see
#   memory feedback_mcp_leaked_project_lock). If your assigned slot is stale/
#   leaked-locked, fall back to ANOTHER verified-free clone (NOT pool4; try
#   pool7/8/9/10...), and record the ACTUAL slot used in the queue row. ALWAYS
#   program_close at end.
#
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUBSYSTEM HINT (not a claim)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   Working hypothesis for all 156: gameplay. The 0x0040-0x0041 region mixes
#   game-state/race-setup logic with boot/menu/save glue, so individual RVAs may
#   reclassify to boot/save/frontend/hud/track. CONFIRM per RVA from decomp and
#   record subsystem_observed=<X> in the queue row when it differs. The sweep +
#   central re-classify reconcile.
################################################################################


================================================================================
Session 1 — bucket_gameplay_00405400_00407620  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool0 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_00405400_00407620/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_al_s1.md  (per-session fragment; sweep merges later)

# Mission
First slice of the gameplay C1 pool (0x00405400-0x00407620). Read each end-to-end
and write a mechanical C2 plate (full control flow, every offset/constant cited,
callees + callers noted). Working hypothesis: gameplay (early game-state / setup).
Confirm or reclassify per RVA.

# Inputs
- Existing C1 plate per RVA: read the path in that RVA's hooks.csv `file` column
  FIRST (deepen, don't restart). Drift-skip any already >= C2.
- Cross-reference: Session 2 (next gameplay range).

# Workflow
1. DUPLICATE-INSTANCE GUARD (above). Then export POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_al_s1";
   use Mashed_pool0 read-only.
2. For each RVA: function_at -> decomp_function (-> listing_code_unit_at if no fn;
   set needs_function_create=<rva> in the queue row if no function object).
3. Write re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_00405400_00407620/<rva>.md with frontmatter
   (rva, name, size_bytes, confidence_target=C2, callees_depth1, callers_noted,
   opened_in_slot, session_date=2026-06-02) + "## Mechanical description" +
   "## Constants" table + "## Uncertainties" (bare [UNCERTAIN], do NOT mint) +
   "## Stubs encountered" (note, do NOT mint).
4. Append ONE row to re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_al_s1.md: date, al_s1, bucket, rvas=<csv of
   plated>, confidence=C1->C2, note=<subsystem_observed + reclassifications +
   library_skip=... + needs_function_create=... + actual pool slot>.
5. One atomic path-scoped commit: "scribe(batch_al s1): author 26 C1->C2 plates
   for gameplay 00405400_00407620".

# Candidates (26)
00405400,00405430,00405540,00405730,00405780,004058b0,004058c0,004058e0,00405920,00405ab0,00405f60,00406070,00406130,00406160,00406370,00406410,004064c0,00406950,004069d0,00406ae0,004074a0,00407550,00407580,004075a0,004075b0,00407620

# STOP-AND-ASK if
- function_at AND listing_code_unit_at both null — surface + skip.
- An RVA is a library family (memcpy/_ftol2/RW primitive) — note in plate, do NOT
  rename; set subsystem_observed=third-party-library[...] + library_skip=<rva> in
  the queue row.


================================================================================
Session 2 — bucket_gameplay_00407640_0040ad40  (promote-c2, 26-RVA drain)
================================================================================

Pool slot:   Mashed_pool1 (pre-assigned — do NOT acquire)
Model:       Opus 4.8 (1M context) (claude-opus-4-8[1m]) — promote-c2 cap 60 (this session: 26)
Skill used:  parallel-fanout promote-c2  (AUTHOR-ONLY)
Bucket dir:  re/analysis/bucket_gameplay_00407640_0040ad40/
Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_al_s2.md

# Mission
Second gameplay slice (0x00407640-0x0040ad40). Working hypothesis: gameplay.
Confirm/reclassify per RVA.

# Workflow
Identical to Session 1, but POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_al_s2", Mashed_pool1,
bucket dir bucket_gameplay_00407640_0040ad40/, queue fragment
re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_al_s2.md, commit "scribe(batch_al s2): author 26 C1->C2 plates
for gameplay 00407640_0040ad40".

# Candidates (26)
00407640,004077e0,00407800,00407a00,00407a20,00407b00,00407b70,00407be0,00407cd0,00407d70,00407db0,00407e20,00408590,00408610,00409300,00409330,004093b0,004095a0,004096a0,00409790,004098a0,004098d0,00409950,00409970,00409a80,0040ad40

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.

Drains to:   re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_al_s6.md

# Mission
Sixth gameplay slice (0x0041a980-0x0041d910). Working hypothesis: gameplay.
Confirm/reclassify per RVA. Closes the lowest-156 slice of the gameplay campaign.

# Workflow
Identical pattern. POOL_SLOT_FILE=".pool_slot_al_s6", Mashed_pool6, bucket dir
bucket_gameplay_0041a980_0041d910/, queue fragment re/SCRIBE_QUEUE_al_s6.md,

# STOP-AND-ASK: same as Session 1.


################################################################################
# END batch_al.txt — 156 RVAs across 6 author-only sessions (gameplay campaign 1/~5).
# After all 6 commit: run the scripted ghidra-sweep on main (see header), then
# central re-classify C1->C2 + merge per-session queue fragments into
# re/SCRIBE_QUEUE.md Drained. Expected: gameplay C1 792 -> ~636 (minus any
# reclassified to boot/save/frontend or vendored-library). Remaining campaigns
# after this slice: gameplay (~636) + render (458).
################################################################################
